game_name: G.I. Joe: A Real American Hero

design_philosophy.core_loop: Select vehicle/character > engage in combat (overhead vehicular or one-on-one melee) > capture Cobra operatives > progress through geographical locations
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Dual-mode combat system requiring adaptation between vehicular overhead gameplay and melee fighting; character selection creates trade-offs (speed vs. power)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command G.I. Joe Special Forces across diverse environments, piloting iconic vehicles and engaging in hand-to-hand combat against Cobra operatives
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid gameplay structure mixing overhead vehicular combat with one-on-one melee encounters; character roster with inherent asymmetric strengths/weaknesses; objective-driven capture mechanic rather than pure elimination; multiplayer integration in a licensed action game

difficulty.lives_system: Character jailing system - too many team members or Cobra operatives imprisoned halts progression; other members are released to perpetuate game
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection provides difficulty modulation through speed/power trade-offs; roster depth (12 playable Joes vs 8 Cobra enemies) allows strategic team composition

progression.upgrade_system: Character roster selection; vehicle choice (Dragonfly, Skystriker, M.O.B.A.T., V.A.M.P.) paired with specific drivers (Ace, Wild Bill, Steeler, Clutch)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Geographic progression through woods, desert, and arctic tundra locations; mandatory capture of eight Cobra operatives gates level completion
replayability_hooks: Twelve distinct playable Joe characters with individual advantages/disadvantages
Four vehicle types with different combat profiles
Eight Cobra opponents with varying difficulty
Character asymmetry encourages replaying with different rosters

world_building_techniques: Geographical diversity (woods, desert, arctic tundra) establishes operational scope
Character-driven roster reflects G.I. Joe team specialization
Cobra Command as unified antagonist faction with eight distinct operatives
Vehicle selection tied to character identity (Duke drives V.A.M.P., etc.)

boss_design.philosophy: Roster-based encounter design; eight Cobra operatives serve as mandatory boss encounters; Zartan includes adaptive mechanic (shapeshifting into Joe character form)
notable_bosses: Zartan - shapeshifter mechanic creates uncertainty about opponent identity

faction_indicators: G.I. Joe Special Forces (12 characters, heroes)
danger_signals: Zartan's identity deception mechanic signals infiltration threat
Environmental hazards in melee sections (obstacles, automated weapons)
environmental_storytelling: Geographical battlefields (woods, desert, arctic) suggest global operations
Vehicle-specific loadouts reflect specialized unit capabilities
Melee arena obstacles create environmental warfare narrative
